data.title: Advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader: Control of the Strait of Hormuz is in Iran's hands
data.description: Ali Velayati, foreign affairs advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader, stated on his personal social media account on the 12th that Iranian diplomacy has always adhered to the principle of "protecting our homeland." He added that control of the Strait of Hormuz is now firmly in Iranian hands. (CCTV News)

data.title: Binance Life's market capitalization surpasses $150 million, with a 24-hour increase of 15.47%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, Binance Life's market capitalization has surpassed $150 million, currently trading at $153.47 million, a 24-hour increase of 15.47%. Odaily reminds users that the price of Meme coin is highly volatile, and investors should participate with caution.

data.title: CryptoQuant Analyst: Long-term Bitcoin holdings continue to grow, signaling a cooling of market speculation.
data.description: CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost stated that the Bitcoin market is currently entering an early stabilization phase, with long-term holding behavior gradually increasing, indicating a potential shift in market structure. Data shows that since October, the number of BTC held for more than a year has increased from approximately 12 million to approximately 12.4 million. While the increase is still relatively modest, it reflects a shift in market behavior towards long-term holding. Since mid-December, the 30-day change has remained consistently positive, indicating that investors are more inclined to continue holding rather than distributing their holdings. Darkfost points out that this change typically signifies a transition from a speculative-driven phase to a phase of stronger long-term conviction. Historically, the proportion of long-term holdings usually declines significantly at market peaks and gradually recovers during bear markets; therefore, the current trend can be seen as an early sign of market stabilization. However, he also emphasizes that this indicator represents a long-term change and typically requires a considerable amount of time to form a clear trend; currently, it is still in its early stages.

data.title: North Korean Crypto Theft Funds Military Expenditures, Experts Warn
data.description: Security experts have highlighted that cryptocurrency theft is increasingly being used to finance North Korea's military activities. According to NS3.AI, researchers have observed a months-long infiltration targeting Drift Protocol, indicating a shift in North Korean operations. These activities have expanded beyond phishing to include attacks on exchanges, wallet services, and key permission holders of DeFi protocols.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs See Strong Inflows as Market Interest Grows
data.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced significant net inflows exceeding $786 million last week, marking their most robust performance since February. According to NS3.AI, BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust attracted approximately $612 million. Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley's newly launched MSBT fund garnered around $46 million within its initial three trading days.

data.title: CLARITY Act Urged for Swift Advancement in Crypto Regulation
data.description: Cynthia Lummis emphasized the urgency of advancing the CLARITY Act, warning that delays could push the next opportunity for establishing a crypto regulatory framework to 2030. According to NS3.AI, notable figures such as David Sacks, Brian Armstrong, and SEC Chairman Paul Atkins have also expressed support for expediting market structure legislation.

data.title: Security experts say North Korea's "open" theft of crypto assets has become a means of financing.
data.description: As North Korean hacking continues to escalate its attacks and infiltration of the crypto industry, security experts point out that a key difference between them and hackers from other countries lies in the fact that crypto assets have become a crucial direct source of funding for North Korea's military spending. Reports indicate that North Korean hackers have once again shaken the industry in their recent months-long infiltration campaign against Drift Protocol. Experts state that this model is not merely a "money transfer tool," but rather a direct "predatory profit-making" scheme used to circumvent international sanctions and obtain readily available hard currency. Security researchers point out that unlike countries like Russia and Iran, North Korea lacks sustainable external economic and commodity export capabilities, thus relying more heavily on crypto theft as a core source of income to support its nuclear weapons and ballistic missile programs. Experts also emphasize that North Korean hackers have expanded their targets from simple phishing attacks to include exchanges, wallet services, and key holders of DeFi protocol permissions, and commonly employ long-term social engineering and identity spoofing infiltration methods. Due to the "irreversible once confirmed" nature of blockchain transactions, the crypto industry is far weaker than the traditional financial system in terms of freezing and recovering funds, making such attacks more destructive in terms of speed and scale. Security experts warn that this type of attack, characterized by "long-term infiltration and precise control takeover," has yet to be effectively addressed by the industry. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Iran responds to Trump: Blocking Iran will not open the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: US President Donald Trump retweeted an article on social media on the 12th about a "naval blockade" of Iran. The Iranian Embassy in Austria responded that same day, saying that a blockade cannot "open" the Strait of Hormuz. The embassy wrote on social media: "To those concerned, a simple explanation: a blockade is a blockade. It cannot 'open' the Strait of Hormuz; it will only restrict it. The only way out of this self-inflicted predicament is to remain open and flexible in negotiations. Follow reason." (Xinhua)

data.title: Google News accidentally displayed Polymarket predictions, which the company officially called a system error.
data.description: Some links to Polymarket's prediction markets briefly appeared in Google News search results, displayed alongside mainstream media news, but were subsequently removed. A Google spokesperson stated that the display was due to a system error, adding that "the website briefly appeared in Google News due to an error and is no longer displayed." Reportedly, before its removal, users discovered that Polymarket's market predictions also appeared below reports from mainstream media outlets such as Reuters and The Guardian in event-driven searches; for example, news and betting market information appeared simultaneously in searches related to "whether ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz." (Cointelegraph)

data.title: US senators warn: The Clarity Act should be passed as soon as possible, otherwise the regulatory window may close until 2030.
data.description: U.S. Senator Cynthia Lummis stated that the U.S. should not continue to delay the legislative process of the CLARITY Act, otherwise it may take nearly four years to further improve the regulatory framework for the crypto industry. She posted on social media platform X, stating, "This is our last chance to pass the CLARITY Act by at least 2030," and emphasizing that "the future of American finance cannot be jeopardized." The bill aims to provide a clearer regulatory structure for the crypto industry, clarifying the responsibilities of regulatory agencies to promote industry innovation and market development. With the U.S. midterm elections approaching, the market is concerned that Congressional priorities may shift, slowing down the legislative process. Former White House AI and Crypto Affairs Director David Sacks also expressed support for expediting the bill, stating, "Now is the time to act," and predicting that the relevant market structure legislation will eventually be signed into law by the president. On the industry front, several figures, including Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong, have recently called for accelerating the legislative process, believing that clear rules will promote innovation and increase market participation. On the regulatory front, SEC Chairman Paul Atkins also expressed support for pushing for comprehensive market structure legislation to avoid continued regulatory uncertainty affecting industry development. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Polymarket's Brief Appearance on Google News Sparks Discussion
data.description:
On April 12, prediction market Polymarket briefly appeared in Google News search results alongside major media outlets like Reuters and The Guardian. According to BlockBeats, this inclusion was quickly removed. A Google spokesperson clarified that the platform's appearance was an error and confirmed that the site is no longer featured in the news section.

Previously, when users searched for event-driven prediction questions, such as whether ships would pass through the Strait of Hormuz, Polymarket's entries appeared directly below traditional news outlets.

Both Polymarket and its competitor Kalshi have been actively pursuing media and platform integration. Last year, Google partnered with these platforms to incorporate their data into the Google Finance section. Additionally, in June of the previous year, X platform announced Polymarket as its official prediction market partner. The Polymarket application has also been integrated into the MetaMask crypto wallet and World App by World.

data.title: Tether CEO: Centralized AI is unsustainable and will lead to a dead end; user autonomy should be restored.
data.description: Paolo Ardoino, CEO of stablecoin issuer Tether, stated in an article on the X platform that "artificial intelligence should be as open as freedom." He believes that the development path of centralized artificial intelligence has reached a "dead end." Against the backdrop of large technology companies continuously building closed AI systems, Tether is pushing for development in the opposite direction, exploring solutions for running AI models on local devices through QVAC, enabling users to control their own data and computing power. This model is more in line with the core concept of "self-custody" in the crypto industry, that is, users should have autonomy over their data and computing resources.

data.title: Tether CEO Advocates for Open AI Development
data.description: Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino has expressed his views on the development of artificial intelligence, stating that AI should be as open as freedom. According to Odaily, Ardoino criticized the centralized approach to AI development, describing it as a 'dead end.' In contrast to the closed AI systems being developed by major tech companies, Tether is exploring alternative methods through QVAC, which focuses on running AI models on local devices. This approach aims to give users control over their data and computing power, aligning with the crypto industry's core principle of self-custody, where users maintain autonomy over their data and computational resources.

data.title: STOCKS | Galaxy Securities Highlights Opportunities Amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description:
On April 12, Galaxy Securities released a report noting that March's Producer Price Index (PPI) turned positive year-on-year, driven by high oil prices and ongoing policy efforts. According to Jin10, the report emphasizes the potential for profit recovery in China's A-share market, particularly in upstream sectors.

The report identifies several investment opportunities. Firstly, it suggests focusing on energy and alternative demand sectors, including coal, coal chemicals, new energy, shipping ports, and oil and gas. Additionally, there is potential for recovery in the non-ferrous metals sector, including precious and minor metals.

Secondly, the report highlights defensive assets such as finance (banks), utilities, and transportation. Thirdly, it points to technology innovation and self-sufficiency sectors, including power equipment, energy storage, semiconductors, computing power, and communication equipment. In the consumer sector, it suggests focusing on agriculture, forestry, animal husbandry, fisheries, food and beverages, and household appliances.

The report also notes that if there are signs of easing in ongoing conflicts, there could be significant recovery opportunities in previously oversold sectors.

data.title: A certain multi-signature address withdrew 31.93 million RAVE tokens from CEX in the past two days, and the asset value has soared to $89.1 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the multi-signature address 0x0A1...790d7, which withdrew a total of 31.93 million RAVE tokens from Bitget in the past two days, has seen its asset value surge from $37.54 million to $89.1 million, an increase of $50 million in two days. On April 8, these tokens were only worth $8.52 million.

data.title: Iranian Parliament Speaker: The United States has understood Iran's logic and principles.
data.description: The Speaker of the Iranian Parliament stated that the Iranian delegation put forward forward-looking initiatives, but ultimately failed to win the trust of the Iranian delegation in this round of negotiations. The US has understood Iran's logic and principles; now it is time for them to decide whether they can win our trust. He thanked Pakistan for its efforts in advancing the negotiation process. (Jin Shi)

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Guotou Silver LOF to Temporarily Suspend Trading on April 13, 2026
data.description: Guotou Silver LOF has announced a temporary suspension of trading starting from the market opening on April 13, 2026, until 10:30 on the same day. According to Jin10, the fund's redemption services will continue to operate as usual during the suspension period. Trading will resume at 10:30 on April 13, 2026.

data.title: CryptoQuant analyst: Continued US-Iran conflict could impact inflation and force the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.
data.description: CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost wrote on the X platform that the current inflation structure in the United States is gradually becoming clearer. Although the March CPI data recorded the largest month-on-month increase since 2022, the core CPI remained basically unchanged, indicating that inflationary pressures have not yet spread comprehensively. This trend needs to be continuously monitored in subsequent PCE data and in the coming months. As long as this structure continues, it means that inflation has not yet become systemically embedded in the US economy, but is more of a phase phenomenon, possibly related to geopolitical conflicts. However, if the US-Iran conflict continues for a long time, inflation may gradually evolve into a systemic risk and begin to affect economic growth. At that time, the Federal Reserve may only be able to deal with it by continuing to raise interest rates.

data.title: Analysts: Bitcoin and Ethereum approaching key price levels may signal a market trend reversal.
data.description: Macro analyst Jordi Visser stated that despite widespread market expectations of a bear market in the crypto sector, Bitcoin and Ethereum are less than 10% away from key levels triggering a trend reversal. He pointed out that if Bitcoin breaks through $76,000 and Ethereum rises above $2,400, it could signal the start of a year-long upward trend. Visser believes the probability of a US recession in the short term is low, a judgment consistent with the pricing of the prediction market Kalshi, which currently estimates a recession probability of approximately 24% in 2026. He also noted that with inflation remaining high and traditional markets (such as the S&P 500) lacking significant growth momentum, funds may continue to flow into asset classes with yield potential. However, market opinions remain divided. Veteran trader Peter Brandt recently stated that Bitcoin's lows for the year may not have been reached yet, and the price may retest or even fall below the $60,000 range. Visser emphasized that the market should not be simply divided by bull and bear cycles, but rather judged from the perspective of longer-term structural and liquidity changes. (Cointelegraph)
